seo.title: Cloudflare AI Gateway vs Portkey vs LiteLLM
seo.description: Compare AI gateways by provider routing, fallbacks, caching, observability, budgets, self-hosting, and operational fit for web apps.

content.intro: An AI gateway centralizes provider credentials, usage logs, routing, retries, limits, and sometimes caching behind one endpoint. That can lower cost and simplify model changes, but it also creates a new critical dependency. Cloudflare AI Gateway, Portkey, and LiteLLM differ most in operating model: edge platform service, managed AI control plane, or self-hostable proxy software.

options.name: Cloudflare AI Gateway
options.tagline: Managed AI gateway integrated with Cloudflare
options.best_for: Web teams already using Cloudflare that want provider access, logging, caching, limits, and routing near their edge stack.

options.name: Portkey
options.tagline: Managed AI gateway and observability platform
options.best_for: Teams wanting a broad managed control plane for routing, guardrails, observability, and provider governance.

options.name: LiteLLM
options.tagline: Open-source OpenAI-compatible proxy for many model providers
options.best_for: Teams that want to self-host a programmable gateway and standardize many providers behind familiar APIs.

criteria.name: Operating model
criteria.description: Who runs the gateway data plane.

values.Portkey: Managed platform with enterprise deployment options
values.LiteLLM: Open-source proxy commonly operated by the application team

criteria.name: Provider abstraction
criteria.description: How clients call multiple model vendors.

values.Cloudflare AI Gateway: Provider-specific and unified compatible endpoints, including Workers AI
values.Portkey: Unified gateway API and provider integrations
values.LiteLLM: OpenAI-compatible request and response translation across many providers

criteria.name: Routing and fallback
criteria.description: Runtime choice of model and recovery from provider failure.

values.Cloudflare AI Gateway: Dynamic routes support conditions, splits, limits, retries, and fallback paths
values.Portkey: Configurable routing, fallbacks, retries, load balancing, and conditional behavior
values.LiteLLM: Router and proxy support fallbacks, retries, load balancing, and deployment groups

criteria.name: Caching
criteria.description: Ability to avoid repeated model calls.

values.LiteLLM: Configurable cache backends and cache behavior

criteria.name: Observability
criteria.description: Visibility into latency, tokens, cost, errors, and request metadata.

values.Cloudflare AI Gateway: Built-in logs and analytics tied to gateway traffic
values.Portkey: Detailed logs, traces, analytics, and feedback-oriented features
values.LiteLLM: Proxy logs, spend tracking, callbacks, and integrations; dashboard behavior depends on deployment

values.Cloudflare AI Gateway: Rate and budget limit elements can participate in dynamic routes
values.Portkey: Usage limits and budgets can be applied through gateway controls
values.LiteLLM: Virtual keys, teams, users, and budgets support spend governance

criteria.name: Self-hosting and control
criteria.description: Ability to run and customize the core gateway yourself.

values.Cloudflare AI Gateway: Managed service rather than a self-hosted gateway
values.Portkey: Managed by default, with deployment options depending on plan
values.LiteLLM: Open-source proxy can run in your own environment

criteria.name: Edge-stack fit
criteria.description: Natural integration with globally distributed web infrastructure.

values.Cloudflare AI Gateway: Native fit with Workers, Workers AI, Secrets Store, and Cloudflare operations
values.Portkey: Works across hosting environments through its gateway endpoint
values.LiteLLM: Portable, but the team owns regional deployment and scaling

criteria.name: Failure planning
criteria.description: The resilience question teams must answer before launch.

values.Cloudflare AI Gateway: Decide whether clients fail closed, bypass the gateway, or use route fallbacks
values.Portkey: Configure fallbacks and define behavior if the managed control plane is unreachable
values.LiteLLM: Operate high availability, persistence, upgrades, and overload protection yourself

verdict.summary: Cloudflare AI Gateway is a natural choice for Cloudflare-hosted apps, Portkey offers a feature-rich managed control plane, and LiteLLM gives engineering teams the most direct operational control. A gateway saves money only when limits, cache eligibility, model routing, and usage attribution are explicit; adding a proxy without those policies merely relocates the API endpoint.

use_cases.scenario: Cloudflare Worker calling several model providers
use_cases.reason: It integrates with the surrounding edge, secrets, logging, and Workers AI environment.

use_cases.scenario: Managed governance across many AI product teams
use_cases.reason: Its gateway, observability, and policy surface reduces the amount of control-plane software a team must operate.
The open-source proxy standardizes providers while keeping deployment and customization under team control.
